name: signed_load_widening
title: Force lh/lb over lhu/lbu by widening extern type to s32
summary: GCC 2.7.2 chooses signed (lh/lb) vs unsigned (lhu/lbu) load by the declared extern type. If our pipeline emits lhu but target has lh, the fix is at the C declaration site -- widen to s32 (or the matching signed shorter type) instead of u8/u16.

structural: Pipeline emits lhu/lbu where target has lh/lb (or vice versa) on the same offset+register
The diff is purely in the load opcode -- imm/registers match

rule_skeleton.instruction: Change `extern u8 g_x;` to `extern s32 g_x;` (or `extern s16` for 16-bit). Then audit sibling files for the same global -- mismatched extern types flip the load between files.

notes: regfix can also force this, but the C-side fix is preferred since it stays consistent across re-runs.
Always grep all .c files for the same symbol -- a sibling file may have the correct type already.
